raw_consumer_statement:
I am disputing a charge of {$640.00} from XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX on XX/XX/year> which I prepaid through a required reservation fee. During the meal, multiple live cockroaches were present in the restaurant, creating an unsafe and unsanitary environment. I immediately reported this to the restaurant staff, who acknowledged the issue. 

While I was not able to photograph the incident myself due to the unsafe conditions and needing to leave immediately, there are public accounts documenting similar issues at this restaurant. For example, a XXXX review posted 9 months ago shows photographs of a live roach on a customers plate. This demonstrates that the unsanitary conditions I experienced are part of a documented pattern of health and safety violations. I am attaching photos of the other customer 's XXXX review so you can see a documented example of the restaurant 's cockroach problem. 

I contacted American Express to dispute the charge, explaining that this was a health and safety issue, not a matter of preference or dissatisfaction. The Amex representative refused to assist in the dispute, insisting I provide photographic proof which was not feasible or appropriate in the moment. Even after explaining that this was a service failure, the representative refused escalation or review by a senior specialist.

This is a clear case where a cardholder was harmed by both the merchants misrepresentation and the credit card companys failure to protect its member.
